semantic.role: Interface definitions for the LLM abstraction layer, establishing the contract between ArchSpine and external language model providers.
responsibilities: Define the UsageInfo structure for tracking token consumption
Define the LLMResponse structure wrapping raw LLM output (json + markdown)
Define the LLMClient interface for executing prompts against a provider
Define the PreviousSemanticContext interface for providing prior analysis state to an LLM call
Define the LLMProvider interface for provider-specific configuration and capabilities
Define the ProviderConfig interface for generic provider settings and authentication
Define the LLMTransport interface for abstracting low-level communication with the LLM endpoint
outOfScope: Any runtime logic including HTTP request handling, caching, or retry policies
Concrete implementations of the LLM interfaces (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic clients)
Prompt construction or policy enforcement (handled by prompt-policy.ts)
invariants: All exported symbols must remain pure type definitions (interfaces or type aliases) – no exported classes, functions, or runtime values
Dependencies must be limited to other type-only modules (e.g., protocol types, prompt policy types) – no runtime dependencies allowed
This module must not import from concrete provider implementations (e.g., OpenAI SDK)

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Define a stable, implementation-agnostic set of interfaces for the LLM layer, enabling multiple provider backends to be swapped without impacting higher-level orchestration code.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Initial commits establishing the foundational LLM type definitions as part of the core ArchSpine mirror system.

publicSurface.description: Represents token usage statistics (input, output, total) returned from an LLM provider call.
Wraps the parsed output of an LLM call, exposing a dynamic JSON payload and a map of markdown-formatted strings, optionally accompanied by token usage info.
Primary interface for executing a prompt via a configured provider and retrieving the LLM response. Consumed by the orchestration layer to drive all LLM interactions.
Carries the prior semantic analysis state into a new LLM call, enabling incremental or diff-based generation requests.
Describes a specific language model provider (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic), including its model name, generation strategy, and prompt task mode. Configures how the LLMClient interacts with that provider.
Generic provider configuration holding API base URL, authentication key, and optional organization identifier. Used by the transport to set up connections.
Abstracts the raw HTTP or socket-level communication with an LLM endpoint, decoupling the transport protocol from the higher-level client logic.
